summary: Justin Brennan teaches how to acquire 10-30 unit multifamily deals with minimal out-of-pocket capital ($20K) by leveraging other people's money (OPM) through structured partnerships. He breaks down deal mechanics, including acquisition fees, limited partner/general partner splits (90/10 capital, 60/40 equity), and how to access off-market deals via brokers and direct-to-owner software. The video emphasizes skill-building in deal-finding, underwriting, financing, and property management as the path to solving large financial problems and earning disproportionate returns.
takeaways: 15-unit deal structure: $1.5M purchase + $100K renovations + $50K closing costs = $1.65M all-in; 70% loan ($1.15M); GP brings 10% ($165K), gets 40% equity + acquisition fee (2-3% = $30-50K); LP brings 90%, gets 60% equity
Net out-of-pocket: $20K after acquisition fee ($50K in - $30K out = $20K net)
Revenue multiples: 3-4 deals in 10-30 unit range produce $10-20K/month passive income, enabling exit from W-2 job
Time commitment: 1-2 hours/day, 5-10 hours/week sufficient to execute (deal sourcing, analysis, offers)
31-unit case study: $19K out-of-pocket → $590K profit after 24-month hold (24-month BRRR refinance, $355K tax-free cash, $2K/month asset mgmt fee, $470K net proceeds from sale)

Deal sourcing: 80% of multifamily market sells pre-market to preferred broker lists (Crexi/LoopNet = bottom 20% only); use direct-to-broker (build relationships on-market) or direct-to-owner (Real Estate Radar software with 25-30M apartment listings, skip-trace, AI likelihood-to-sell)
Tax strategy: Debt is tax-free money (not earned income); bonus depreciation + cost segregation can eliminate taxes for years ('zero taxes for last 8 years' example)

skill_candidates.change: Learn to quickly underwrite deals using standardized spreadsheets to identify money-makers vs. money-pits before writing offers
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin emphasizes 'analyze them quickly to determine if it's a good deal' as core skill #2 of 5; mentions live deal analysis/underwriting at workshop; notes that most brokers' Crexi/LoopNet deals don't make sense ('do the deals make sense or do they not make sense? They do not')

skill_candidates.change: Build relationships with off-market deal flow via direct-to-broker approach (tour on-market properties, request preferred list access) rather than relying solely on Crexi/LoopNet
skill_candidates.evidence: 'The direct-to-broker approach leverages the broker's on-market listings from LoopNet and Crexi to get access to their off-market opportunities...80% of the market is right here and sells right here. It never makes it to that Crexi and LoopNet, ever...this is why you're frustrated'

skill_candidates.change: Execute weekly action cadence: 1-2 hours daily on deal sourcing, analysis, and offer writing; treat as non-negotiable 'passion project' outside W-2 job for 2-3 years until passive income reaches exit threshold
skill_candidates.evidence: 'One to two hours per day, five to 10 hours a week, you'll execute the game...I was a realtor working 8-10 hours a day...8:30 rolls around...I am making things happen for my family. I'm on the computer trying to find deals, analyze them, write offers...within 2-3 years, out'

skill_candidates.change: Master three-element capital-raising formula: (1) deal that makes financial sense, (2) pitch deck explaining deal + investor returns, (3) process for legally taking capital and depositing to property account
skill_candidates.evidence: 'You need three things to raise money. You need a deal that makes financial sense. You need a pitch deck that helps explain what the deal is and what they get for their money. And then lastly, a process when somebody says, Hey, I want to give you 25 grand'

title: How I Turned $100K Into $3.5M With Apartments
